Repository: getsentry/sentry
Release: 25.2.0
Tag: 25.2.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2025-02-18T19:23:53Z; created_at: 2025-02-15T17:04:44Z
Release notes:
### Automate Code Mappings & In-App Stack Trace Rules for Java projects (ongoing)
Currently, we ask developers to set up their Java code mappings manually (because the SDK cannot determine it):
https://docs.sentry.io/platforms/java/source-context/#setting-up-code-mappings
Unfortunately, many stack traces do not contain in-app frames, thus, many Sentry features are not available (e.g. SCM linking, suspect commit).
This tracks using the code mappings derivation system to automatically add the code mappings and stack trace rules to mark in-app frames as such.
This will automatically enable the following Sentry features :
- Suspect Commits
- GitHub pull request comments
- Auto-assignments (if Codeowners imported)
- Stack trace linking
- Code coverage (if the customer also has an account)
This will only be available to customers with the GitHub integration installed.
You can read more about the system here:
https://blog.sentry.io/code-mappings-and-why-they-matter/
By: @armenzg (#85174, #85065)
### Various fixes & improvements
- Revert "feat(autofix): Add Autofix status to sidebar button (#85287)" (b20e50ed) by @getsentry-bot
- fix(autofix): Fix flash on initial run (#85299) by @roaga
- feat(autofix): Add Autofix status to sidebar button (#85287) by @roaga

- fix(auto_source_config): Handle when the frame is None (#85235) by @armenzg
- feat(issues): Disable delete on missing permission (#85278) by @scttcper
- fix(widget-builder): Visualize field overflows on long field names (#85271) by @nikkikapadia
- feat(nav): Update rest of alert links to use new pathname builder (#85257) by @malwilley
- fix(profiling): Prevent landing page from scrolling on context menu (#85273) by @Zylphrex
- ref: remove default margin from Alert in sentry (#85128) by @michellewzhang
- feat(taskworker) Add metrics for RPC durations (#85194) by @markstory
- feat(nav): Update alert links from explore/insights/crons (#85254) by @malwilley
- feat(nav): Update replay onboarding to use drawer (#85267) by @malwilley
- ref(crons): Rename parallel field -> batched_parallel (#85261) by @evanpurkhiser
- Reapply "ref(crons): Remove parallel mode (batched-parallel replaced it)" (#85262) by @evanpurkhiser

- ref: get_group_{body,title,description} to not need request (#85263) by @asottile-sentry

Additionally, the release includes various fixes and improvements such as reverting and fixing autofix status sidebar button, fixing flash on initial run in autofix, left aligning commit authors in issues, adding support for sentry apps for issue alerts in NOA in aci, implementing an all checks page for crons, fixing typo in user feedback api, namespacing Widget components in dashboards, removing config_topic from UptimeRegionConfig in uptime, adding ability to ignore useless filenames in seer grouping, handling when the frame is None in auto_source_config, disabling delete on missing permission in issues, fixing visualize field overflows on long field names in widget-builder, updating rest of alert links to use new pathname builder in nav, preventing landing page from scrolling on context menu in profiling, removing default margin from Alert in sentry, adding metrics for RPC durations in taskworker, updating alert links from explore/insights/crons in nav, updating replay onboarding to use drawer in nav, renaming parallel field to batched_parallel in crons, reapplying removal of parallel mode in crons, extracting selection components in widget-builder, publishing GroupExternalIssue endpoint in apis, and refactoring get_group_body/title/description to not need request.

In Autofix, features include allowing non seat based seer to skip setup, switching inspection to single llm call using gemini flash, autofix introspection analytics, adding UI labels for missing AutofixReferrer values, and rendering line numbers in autofix evidence. In Cells, features include removing cross-org feature gating from notification settings and adding cell-routing mode to devservices. In Cmdk, features include adding Open in Production and Open in Development actions, freezing visible action list during keyboard navigation, and adding project search action to command palette. In Conversations, features include adding copy conversation as markdown button, swapping badge from alpha to beta, and adding Amplitude analytics to conversation pages. In Dashboards, features include adding span-first support for web vital dashboard, validating display type against dataset config, requiring metric_unit in AI tracemetrics aggregates, and teaching AI dashboard generator the tracemetrics aggregate format. In Explore, features include heatmap tooltip trace links, linking to aggregates from dropdown, and adding Heat Map widget to Explore metrics. In Github Enterprise, features include adding frontend pipeline steps for GHE integration setup, adding API-driven pipeline backend for GHE integration setup, and allowing github.com as a source for the GitHub Enterprise integration. In Issues, features include bringing back SEER_PR_CREATED activity creation and hiding from timeline, two-column activity icons and colors, unifying issue activity streams, adding activity feed v2 flag, consolidating activity comment input, and replacing DebugMeta store with context.

questions.query_text: In the getsentry/sentry releases, which release introduces the ability to create new environments in the API and adds a 'moved' badge to the old alerts nav item?

answer_support.answer_text: adding ability to create new environments in the API, adding 'moved' badge to old alerts nav item

questions.query_text: Across the getsentry/sentry releases, how does the approach to alerting in release 26.2.0 differ from the approach in release 26.4.0?

options.text: 26.2.0 focuses on adding issue type conditions and publishing workflow engine API docs, while 26.4.0 focuses on adding onboarding banners and inline environment creation.
26.2.0 removes all alerting features, while 26.4.0 reintroduces them.
Both releases only update dashboard widgets without any alerting changes.
26.2.0 deprecates the workflow engine, while 26.4.0 removes it entirely.

questions.expected_answer: 26.2.0 focuses on adding issue type conditions and publishing workflow engine API docs, while 26.4.0 focuses on adding onboarding banners and inline environment creation.

answer_support.answer_text: adding issue type condition to new Alerts UI, publishing workflow engine API docs
adding onboarding banner, adding ability to create new environments in the API

questions.query_text: In the getsentry/sentry release window, order the following release-content states from earliest to latest: (1) a release that adds support for streamed output in autofix and configures workflow notifications by group type for metric-issues, (2) a release that adds a 'new' badge for logs in the sidebar and flattens visualize yaxes into multiple in explore, (3) a release that adds a warning message when action is not compatible with metric detectors and adds sorting for connected alerts, (4) a release that adds inline alert creation to monitor details page and adds admin audit log tab to customer details view.

answer_support.answer_text: adding support for streamed output in autofix, configuring workflow notifications by group type for metric-issues
adding a new badge for logs in the sidebar, flattening visualize yaxes into multiple in explore
adding warning message when action is not compatible with metric detectors, adding sorting for connected alerts
adding inline alert creation to monitor details page, adding admin audit log tab to customer details view
